title: Object Types, Interfaces, Aliases, and Structural Compatibility · Stakeholder TypeScript Mission
summary: A civic data team. Maintainers need literal-safe, evolvable records and unknown-input validation without unsafe assertions. Planned artifact: Deliver Strict Public Service Contract Library with acceptance evidence, rubric defense, reflection, and a changed-constraint extension. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Control literal widening with const bindings, readonly context, const assertions, and const type parameters.
Use satisfies to check a value against a contract while retaining useful inferred literal information.
Write boolean predicates and type predicates whose runtime checks actually justify their claimed types.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.typescript:
// typescript-basics cumulative strict TypeScript portfolio.
// Add one typed behavior, runtime boundary, changed case, and evidence return per activity.

steps.title: Commit to the mission model
steps.instruction:
project case — A scheduling record has id "job-6", an optional owner, readonly creation time, and a mutable status transition. Reject a misspelled status field, protect the invariant, and show which readonly promise exists only at compile time.

steps.title: Defend the stakeholder delivery

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Predict structural assignability while identifying where extra members, methods, and variance create risk.
